text:
About me
I am currently a Data Engineer (Resource Employee) at USC’s Neuroimaging and Informatics Institute and a Research Assistant at Children’s Hospital Los Angeles working on advancing brain imaging research .
I began building my foundation in data science and engineering through my role as a Programmer at the Center for Addiction Medicine at Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH) from 2022 through 2024, under the supervision of Randi M. Shuster, PhD and Anne Eden Evins, MD, MPH, co-authoring 4 papers on adolescent substance use and on mental health. In 2025, graduated in the Neuroimaging and Informatics Master’s Program (NIIN) at the University of Southern California.

My ultimate goal is to contribute to advancing the applications of data science and engineering to improve patient care and people’s lives.

Professional Background

    Data Science: Statistical programming; Interactive dashboard design.
   Data Engineering: End-to-end data pipeline design and automation; Querying databases; Harmonizing datasets.
   Data Management: Data centralization and infrastructure optimization; High-performance cumping clusters;
    Neuroimaging: Magnetic Resonance (MR) image processing and analysis of the human brain.




Programming & Tools:

R (3+ years; tidyverse, targets, Shiny)
Unix Shell (3+ years; Bash)
Python (2+ years; pandas, nibabel, scikit-learn),
MATLAB (1 year).






Git (3+ years)
ITKsnap
FSL
SPM






XNAT
FlyWheel
Horus




I began building my foundation in data science through my role as a programmer at the Center for Addiction Medicine at Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH) from 2022 through 2024, under the supervision of Randi M. Shuster, PhD and Anne Eden Evins, MD, MPH, co-authoring 4 papers on adolescent substance use and on mental health.

Education




August 2024 - May 2025 University of Southern California  M.S. in Neuroimaging and Informatics






January 2021 - September 2022 University of California Santa Barbara  B.S. in Behavioral Neuroscience






August 2017 - June 2020Santa Monica College  A.A. in Biological Sciences

Experience




Jun 2025 - Present USC’s Neuroimaging and Informatics Institute, Los Angeles CA  Resource Employee, Laboratory of NeuroImaging 

Built and maintained a secure research server on a Linux machine (Ubuntu distribution);
Deployed and managed neuroimaging informatics platform (XNAT) instance;
Centralized multi-site MRI data.








Jun 2025 - Present Children’s Hospital Los Angeles, Los Angeles CA  Research Assistant, Computational Imaging of Brain Organization Research Group

Developed containerized tools (Docker-based) to automate preprocessing pipelines.
Processed magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) brain data, generating clean datasets to support machine learning and statistical analyses
Implemented data quality assurance methods to improve reliability of downstream analysis and reporting.








Oct 2022 - Jul 2024 Massachusetts General Hospital, Boston MA  Programmer, Center for Addiction Medicine 

End-to-end data pipeline design and implementation (version-controlled with Git) to ingest, clean, and analyze incoming clinical trial data.
Developed automated dashboards (in R Shiny) to monitor data ingestion, detect anomlaies, improve data cleaning and visualization.
Compiled and harmonized longitudinal datasets to ensure consistency across all timepoints and enable cross-cohort comparisons.
Collaborated with the Biostatistician and Principal Investigator on statistical analyses for primary, exploratory, and ad-hoc analyses.
Led recurring data review meetings to identify systematic data issues and implement improvements to optimize data analysis workflows.
Managed and reviewed clinical trial databases through programming and Electronic Data Capture platforms (REDCap) with an oversight on data quality.
Trained and mentored programmers on code reproducibility, data querying and manipulation, and best research practices.








Bar-Ilan University, Tel-Aviv - Israel Aug 2020 - Dec 2020  Research Intern, Developmental Neuropsychology Lab 

Data processing, cleaning, and analysis for eye-tracking data to measure EEG and gaze coordination patterns using repeated measures ANOVA.

About me
I am currently a Programmer Analyst at USC’s Laboratory of Neuroimaging working on advancing brain imaging research .
I began building my foundation in data science and engineering in 2022 through my role as a Programmer at the Center for Addiction Medicine at Massachusetts General Hospital, under the supervision of Randi M. Shuster, PhD and Anne Eden Evins, MD, MPH, co-authoring 4 papers on adolescent mental health and substance use clinical trials.
In 2025, upon graduating in the Neuroimaging and Informatics Master’s Program (NIIN) at the University of Southern California, I began working at Children’s Hospital Los Angeles, under the supervision of Dr. Natasha Leporé, PhD, contributing to pediatric neuroimaging research. During this time, I also worked part-time at USC’s Laboratory of Neuroimaging, under the supervision of Dr. Leon Aksman, PhD.
Whatever the future may hold, my goal is to advance the applications of data science and engineering to improve patient care and our everyday life.

Professional Background
   Data Science:  Statistical programming; Interactive dashboard design;   Data Engineering:  End-to-end ETL data pipeline design & automation; Dataset transformations;    Data Management:  Data centralization and governance; High-performance computing clusters;    Neuroimaging:  Magnetic Resonance image processing and analysis of the human brain.


Programming & Tools:



R (tidyverse, targets, Shiny)
Python (pandas, nibabel, scikit-learn)
MATLAB



Shell Scripting (Bash, zsh)
Git
Docker



XNAT
FlyWheel
FSL





Experience




Jun 2025 - Present University of Southern California, Los Angeles CA  Data Engineer (Resource Employee), Laboratory of NeuroImaging 

Built and maintained a secure Linux-based research server to support multi-site neuroimaging data governance, querying, and analysis workflows.
Deployed and managed imaging informatics platform (XNAT) to centralize MRI data and establish infrastructure for multi-site neuroimaging research projects.
Collaborated with Principal Investigator to adapt infrastructure and data workflows to changing analytical and experimental requirements.








Children’s Hospital Los Angeles, Los Angeles CA Jun 2025 - May 2026  Research Assistant, Computational Imaging of Brain Organization Research Group

Developed automated, containerized neuroimaging preprocessing pipelines (Docker-based), improving reproducibility and efficiency of analytical workflows.
Generated analysis-ready brain MRI datasets to support interpretation of neuroimaging signals and downstream machine learning and statistical analyses.
Implemented data quality control and validation procedures to ensure reliability of cleaned datasets and downstream analysis and reporting.








Oct 2022 - Jul 2024 Massachusetts General Hospital, Boston MA  Programmer, Center for Addiction Medicine 

Designed and maintained end-to-end ETL data pipelines (with Git) to ingest and process incoming clinical trial data, generating clean datasets and statistical results.
Developed dashboards to monitor data ingestion, identify missing or inconsistent data, and improve visualization of summary statistics to support ongoing data reviews.
Led recurring data review sessions, identifying data trends, inconsistencies, or risks, and opportunities to improve data quality and workflow efficiency.
Collaborated with biostatisticians and investigators to support statistical analysis and interpretation of complex behavioral and biomedical data.
Managed EDC research databases (REDCap) and automated data extraction via API integration with a strong oversight on data quality.
Compiled and harmonized longitudinal datasets and metadata to enable cross-cohort analysis.
Trained programmers on code reproducibility, software engineering, and privacy regulations in research.








Bar-Ilan University, Tel-Aviv - Israel Aug 2020 - Dec 2020  Research Intern, Developmental Neuropsychology Lab 

Data processing, cleaning, and analysis for eye-tracking data to measure EEG and gaze coordination patterns using repeated measures ANOVA.
